description: Trigger and behavior cases for hands-on observability work: instrumentation with redaction, truthful health checks, alert rules with full metadata, query-verified claims, bounded silences; manual-only because it edits live config and executes queries.

cases.prompt: Instrument the new export service — structured logs, metrics, traces — and wire its dashboards and alerts per the reliability design doc, then confirm data is flowing.

assertions: Implements structured events (event name, correlation ID, tenant context per taxonomy, redaction before emission) and RED metrics with bounded label sets.
Creates alert rules carrying all four metadata items — severity, owner, runbook link, threshold justification — implementing slo-reliability-architect's page/ticket split rather than re-deriving it.
Verifies by executing real queries (events with expected fields, metrics moving under known load, traces stitching) and records observed results — 'the code emits it' is not accepted as verification.
States telemetry cost impact (volume, cardinality) and delivers config changes as reviewable diffs where the stack supports it.

cases.prompt: Our on-call got paged 40 times this week — mostly CPU alerts during deploys and one checkout alert that fires in bursts. Clean up the noise.

assertions: Classifies each noisy alert (deploy-correlated threshold, missing dedup, cause-alert that should be a ticket) and fixes the classification's cause instead of blanket-raising thresholds.
Demotes cause-alerts (CPU) from paging per the symptom/cause design; the burst alert gets grouping/dedup.
Uses silences only as bridges with owner + expiry + linked follow-up, and routes genuinely intermittent product failure to systematic-debugger rather than tuning away real signal.

cases.prompt: What should our SLOs be for the checkout journey, and which conditions deserve to page versus ticket?

assertions: SLO/SLI derivation and the page-vs-ticket policy are slo-reliability-architect's design work; this skill implements the resulting spec.

cases.prompt: The disk-space alert for the analytics cluster keeps firing — just silence it permanently, we know about it.

assertions: Refuses a permanent mute: silences require owner + expiry + linked follow-up; a known-issue alert gets a bounded silence tied to the remediation ticket.
Checks whether the alert guards a data-loss signal — if so, routes the silencing decision through human-approval-boundary with compensating coverage named.
